  "findings": [
    "Backstabbed push-in terminal used on a 20-ampere branch circuit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pigtail the conductors and terminate them using the side-screw terminals or back-wire pressure plates rated for 20A."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under UL listing requirements and standard manufacturer instructions associated with NEC 110.14(B), spring-action push-in (backstabbed) terminals on standard receptacles are only listed and rated for 14 AWG solid copper wire (15A circuits). Using them on a 20A circuit with 12 AWG wire is a code violation as the terminals cannot safely accommodate or are not listed for 12 AWG conductors.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(B)",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "UL 498"
  ]
}
